channel.title: /home/mole
channel.description: Creations and ramblings of Mikael "Molentum" Myyrä.

item.title: Of elves and worlds
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Today is the two year anniversary of the character design of Miriel, in a sense also marking the beginning of the Yon project. At this point I can confidently say this was the best thing I've ever done for my art and a shockingly impactful influence on my life in general. It'll require me to get a little personal and emotional, but I want to talk about why.

item.title: Hey what's up
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Salma's the type of guy to greet you from a random tree that isn't even near a body of water just because he can and thinks it's funny.

item.title: Catching rays
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Mermay + meme pose (originating from a photo by Marina Choy) + experimental shading and composition style. I like how it turned out.

item.title: Salma
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Salma is a bit of a "lone wolf" of a merman, often found fishing or just wandering the waters by his lonesome. He's quieter and less flashy than most of his compatriots, but still enjoys a bit of fashion (he particularly likes his tattoos) and sometimes sings when he thinks nobody is listening.

item.title: Solitude
item.author: Unknown
item.description: A little painting for an exhibition organized by the local university students' art club. Its theme is "emotions", and naturally I chose my favorite of them all, that sense of absolute peace you can only feel when completely alone and in no hurry to go anywhere or do anything (for which, curiously, there's no word in the Finnish language, but "solitude" describes it well).

item.title: What is local color?
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Local color is a concept we all understand intuitively. Roses are red, violets are blue. We perceive things as having colors in some intrinsic sort of way. However, this property of things is not actually a color but something subtly different. Understanding the difference gives us a powerful way to think about color in painting.

item.title: Getting sparkly
item.author: Unknown
item.description: An experiment using Schmincke aquarelle bronze powder mixed with gouache. It doesn't glitter so much in this scan, but you'll just have to believe me that it's quite fun to look at under a strong light.

item.title: Matching lace
item.author: Unknown
item.description: A mixed media painting with gouache and ink, presenting for your consideration the idea of matching tattoo and lace pattern.

item.title: Fountain drinks
item.author: Unknown
item.description: A gouache and digital mixed media piece that I entered for the graphic art compo at Instanssi 2026. I also participated in the "fast graphics" compo with a time limit of 90 minutes and theme of "crab society" with the following ink sketch, titled "Ravunsilmälinssi" / "Crab's eye lens", which won second place (🥳):

item.title: Finding my artistic voice
item.author: Unknown
item.description: "Finding one's voice" is a phrase that doesn't admit a rigorous definition but feels like an accurate description of my current moment with visual art. I've been thinking about why I relate so strongly with these words right now, and wanted to dump some of those thoughts into a brief blog post.

item.title: Siren's gaze
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Many generations have passed since the merfolk ended the practice of using seduction to procure food, but Aava still knows the art of the Look 👁 👁

item.title: Stuff I liked in 2025
item.author: Unknown
item.description: It's time for another year-end list of good stuff. Metal records, books, and games I enjoyed in 2025, with the music restricted to new releases. Also some personal retrospecting at the end.

item.title: Christmas greeting card 2025
item.author: Unknown
item.description: The tradition continues, now for the first time in gouache.

item.title: Haze
item.author: Unknown
item.description: An experiment in minimalism using transparent gouache.

item.title: Back home
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Happy to report that Miriel made it back from her Inktober adventures in one piece.

item.title: Inktober 2025
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Another Inktober where I participated every other day; great fun as always! Featuring a little substory about Miriel's trip to an abandoned city, some less serious doodles depending on the mood of the day, and at least one appearance of every character I've designed so far.

item.title: Stuff with gouache
item.author: Unknown
item.description: I've recently started learning to paint traditionally with gouache. Here's a few of my first personal paintings, presented in chronological order to show my progress, of which I'm quite proud so far :)

item.title: Art Fight 2025
item.author: Unknown
item.description: I participated in Art Fight for the first time, where I semi-accidentally ended up challenging myself to make as many drawings as I can without ever repeating a style. I've compiled my "attacks" and "defenses" on this page. Big thanks to everyone involved for making such lovely art and allowing me to post it!

item.title: Art rules from first principles, part 1: Perspective
item.author: Unknown
item.description: There are many shorthands and rules of thumb one encounters when learning the fundamentals of representational art — vanishing points, warm-cool contrasts, grouping of values into light, midtone and shadow, etc. There are many good non-technical explanations of these ideas out there, but I wanted to dive deeper and investigate how they arise from the physics of light and geometry of space. In this first part we'll look at geometric interpretations of common perspective drawing methods.

item.title: Mermay inks (2025)
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Portraits of Aava drawn with ink on paper during the month of May, featuring some experimental use of colored inks.

item.title: Big fancy window
item.author: Unknown
item.description: The merfolk's architecture is just as needlessly dramatic as their fashion.

item.title: Aava
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Merfolk are uniquely well suited for the study of marine biology (on account of the ability to stay underwater indefinitely), so many of their institutes of higher learning are focused on the topic. Aava is one young researcher in the field and an amateur sculptor, with an ostentatious fashion sense typical of her people.

item.title: Basil
item.author: Unknown
item.description:
Olivia and Basil are fauns, siblings, and mathematicians. They are inseparable, often working together and always getting up to hijinks together. They're highly competitive, egging each other on into more and more difficult challenges and absurd pranks.

Basil is olivia's little brother, slightly less academically gifted but making up for it in attitude and work ethic. He's also the wilder of the two personalities, usually the inciting force when they get into trouble.

item.title: Olivia
item.author: Unknown
item.description:
Olivia and Basil are fauns, siblings, and mathematicians. They are inseparable, often working together and always getting up to hijinks together. They're highly competitive, egging each other on into more and more difficult challenges and absurd pranks.

Olivia is Basil's older sister, two years ahead in her studies and usually the victor in their academic contests. She's more reserved in personality than her brother, though, and doesn't always go along with his wildest ideas.

item.title: Olivia & Basil
item.author: Unknown
item.description: More imaginary nerds for my silly little worldbuilding project. Olivia and her little brother Basil, mathematicians who also happen to be fauns.

item.title: Painting normal maps, and other Krita tips
item.author: Unknown
item.description: I'm a big fan of using software effectively and optimizing my workflows, so over my years using Krita I've spent quite a bit of time digging into its features. In this post I want to talk about how I use this wonderful piece of software and highlight some not-so-obvious bits that have made a difference in my process. I'll do general painting first, hopefully in a way that is useful to beginners, and then indulge in some nerding out about normal maps and how to make them.

item.title: Night out
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Sometimes you just want to paint a sexy pinup with no further story or justification and that's ok (but then it gets a story anyway because you don't know how to do abstract backgrounds, and that's ok too)

item.title: Elegil
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Gil was born to the same tribe of elves as Miriel, but he never quite meshed with their rugged lifestyle. He was a scatterbrained dreamer of a kid, preferring to dance and make up stories rather than swim and race with the other kids. Soon after coming of age, he moved to the city (name and geography TBD), where he dances with one of the local theaters and works at a community kitchen.

item.title: 1910s dress-up
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Some historical dresses from the 1910s (found via The Dreamstress; original sources are various museums) worn by Miriel because why not. Working my way out of a slump with some comfort zone portraits.

item.title: A decade of digital art
item.author: Unknown
item.description: The oldest digital drawing I have on my computer turns ten years old today. This post is a self-indulgent trip down memory lane, looking back at landmark pieces and lessons learned throughout the years.

item.title: Game graphics: the journey so far
item.author: Unknown
item.description: The majority of my recent game development work has revolved around graphics — both workflows for making them and technology for rendering them. Many ideas have been had and many scrapped along the way, and the journey certainly isn't over yet, but I think I'm moving towards something pretty unique and cool. This is a devlog-style post where I meander through some of the more noteworthy moments so far without going very deep into technical details (but providing technical sources where relevant).

item.title: Rounding collider corners
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Back in 2022 I entirely rewrote one of the most complicated parts of Starframe, the collision detection system. A notable feature of the new system is the ability to give any shape rounded corners. I also wrote most of this post back then, but things happened and it was left unfinished. Here it is now, more than two years later. Better late than never I suppose.

item.title: Stuff I liked in 2024
item.author: Unknown
item.description: In recent years I've made a habit of posting a list of my favorite heavy metal releases of the year on Mastodon. This year I decided to list more than just music and do it in a more permanent place here on my website. So here are some metal albums, books, and games I enjoyed in 2024.

item.title: Blooming
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Ink sketches exploring the relationship between human and nature through varying degrees of body horror.

item.title: Very important application of dryad magic
item.author: Unknown
item.description: The dryads are capable of powerful acts of creation and destruction, an important consequence of which is the ability to cook a marshmallow anywhere, at any time.

item.title: Ivu
item.author: Unknown
item.description:
The dryads, Ivu amongst them, are caretakers of the forest and some of the most powerful magical beings in the world, wielding shapeshifting growth and destructive fire to drive nature's cycles of death and rebirth ever onward.

Ivu is the avatar of the birch tree and also the protagonist of my long-term game project Flamegrower — in fact, the dryads' magic powers were created out of the game's mechanics.

item.title: Inktober 2024
item.author: Unknown
item.description: I did Inktober every other day again this year, incorporating colored inks for the first time to varying levels of success. I used the travel-themed prompts to explore Miriel's adventures and develop the world she lives in.

item.title: Of the Soil
item.author: Unknown
item.description: An experiment with abstract color and composition choices with rough sketchy brushwork, probably the first of many.

item.title: Ten formative games
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Inspired by recent discussions in the Eggplant Show Discord community, here's a list of ten video games. Not my favorite games (though many of them are that too), but ones that had a notable influence on who I am today.

item.title: Summer night at the lake
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Inspired by a late evening swim I had recently.

item.title: Miriel
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Miriel of the hunter-gatherer elves living in the northern forests of Yon. She's adept with the bow, her people's traditional weapon, and spends her summers hunting, fishing, and foraging with a small group of her fellows. She enjoys adventure and solitude, often breaking away from the group for solo expeditions. When not out in the wild she likes to cook, read, and dress up in fancy dresses and jewelry, which she sometimes crafts herself. She also appreciates art and often spends idle evenings drawing or whittling wood.

item.title: Mermaid but l o n g
item.author: Unknown

item.title: Don't forget to charge your moon staff
item.author: Unknown

item.title: Demodemonini
item.author: Unknown
item.description: My submission for the graphic art compo at Instanssi 2024. Lines drawn in ink on paper, characters painted digitally with Krita, and animated effects written in Rust with wgpu. Source code on Codeberg

item.title: Heather
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Continuing my series of flower-themed, vaguely Art Nouveau -inspired illustrations from 2022 (previous entries: Lily of the Valley, Poppy, Cornflower, Marsh Marigold).

item.title: Self-portrait 2023
item.author: Unknown
item.description: New self-portrait and profile picture to show off my improved skills since the last one from 2020.

item.title: Christmas greeting card 2023
item.author: Unknown
item.description: My annual Christmas greeting card, this time using traditional media.

item.title: Molenia of the Large Sword
item.author: Unknown
item.description: This character again, this time from a recent Dark Souls 3 playthrough.

item.title: Inktober 2023
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Quick drawings done every other day for Inktober 2023, all made with a Pentel Pocket Brush brush pen.

item.title: Wandering Sharpshooter
item.author: Unknown
item.description: My recurring RPG character Molenia (also featured in Irithyll Battlemage and Nox Dragonslayer), this time in their Diablo IV rogue gear.

item.title: Mermay 2023
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Sketches exploring the idea of "wouldn't it be funny if merfolk wore regular human clothes" for this year's Mermay.

item.title: Demovelhon työpaja
item.author: Unknown
item.description: An exercise in combining 3D modelling and digital painting. The title is Finnish for "Demowizard's Workshop". This was my entry for the graphic art compo at local demoparty Instanssi, where it won second place!

item.title: Crystal Reaper
item.author: Unknown
item.description: A "quick" doodle that I accidentally thought about too much and turned into a whole concept with multiple characters.

item.title: Infernal Butterfly
item.author: Unknown
item.description: In an edgy dark fantasy kind of mood, trying out an edgy style to match.

item.title: Marsh Marigold
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Flower series, part 4. Simultaneously experimenting with rougher, more textured painting and cleaner decorative shapes using vector tools. There's a certain tension between lines and painting that I'm finding very interesting.

item.title: Cornflower
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Flower series, part 3. Continuing to challenge my sense of values with a strongly lit scene, as well as trying to push the complexity of my backgrounds.

item.title: Poppy
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Flower series, part 2. A challenging lighting scenario that taught me a great deal about values, with the help of valuable feedback from some artists on Discord.

item.title: Lily of the Valley
item.author: Unknown
item.description: The beginning of an Art Nouveau -inspired series based on flowers. Aiming to learn the shape language of plants and improve my abstract decorative designs.

item.title: Late Night Dip
item.author: Unknown
item.description: My standard art process: painting a cool background to distract from the fact that I just felt like drawing a pretty lady.

item.title: Bloodmage Mermaid
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Another cheesy murder-mermaid for Mermay 2022.

item.title: Heart Hands
item.author: Unknown
item.description: A cheesy murder-mermaid for Mermay 2022.

item.title: Nox Dragonslayer
item.author: Unknown
item.description: My player character from Elden Ring with her coolest gear.

item.title: Irithyll Battlemage
item.author: Unknown
item.description: My player character from Dark Souls 3 with her coolest gear.

item.title: Isidora, Voice of the Dead
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Fanart of my favorite boss from Blasphemous.

item.title: Starframe devlog: Rope physics
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Starframe's rope physics is the first piece of physics code I've written that I designed entirely myself. This is a short story of that design process and the challenges and solutions found along the way.

item.title: Magmerm
item.author: Unknown
item.description: A fun quick design for Mermay 2021.

item.title: Starframe devlog: Solving Constraints in Physics
item.author: Unknown
item.description: The Starframe physics solver has gone through a few iterations recently as my understanding of the problem and knowledge of available methods have grown. In this post I attempt to explain what constraints are and detail a few game-engine-friendly ways of solving them. I'll try to make this understandable to someone with approximately the level of knowledge I had before starting this: decent linear algebra and calculus skills and a tiny bit of physics knowledge.

item.title: Self-portrait 2020
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Celebrating three years of daily drawing practice. A breakthrough in terms of style for me, introducing many features I still use two years later.

item.title: Starframe devlog: Architecture
item.author: Unknown
item.description: I've spent a large portion of the past two years writing and rewriting the basic structures that describe a game and its content in Starframe (formerly known as MoleEngine). These are some notes on what I've done so far and what I've learned from it.

item.title: You're not afraid of the dark, are you?
item.author: Unknown
item.description: For Mermay 2020. This was a huge leap in skill for me, both in linework and painting.

item.title: Biker Santa
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Christmas greeting card for 2018, first one of these I ever made.

item.title: Starframe devlog: Introduction
item.author: Unknown
item.description: Starframe is an ambitious hobby project, still early on in development, aiming to create a general-purpose 2D game engine suitable for rapid prototyping and game jams as well as more polished, hopefully commercial-quality products. This blog is meant to document the development and ideas behind it in a fair bit of detail - mostly to organize and revise my own thoughts, but with the hope that someone else might also find it interesting and/or helpful. In this first post I will outline what exactly I am trying to accomplish and why I am doing it.
